groups.title: Oyster Bay Sailing Club Courses
groups.desc:
Questions 1-6
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

table.title: Oyster Bay Sailing Club Courses
content: basic theory e.g. understanding the $22148$ and tides; basic sailing skills including $22149$ information

£200 
 $22150$ available for club members 
  all inclusive (plus a useful $22151$
a $22152$ at the end of the course for all participants

questions.analysis: 题目要求填写Taster Day课程小组的最大人数。原文中女士提到“usually about eight people but no more than ten”，明确指出最多不超过10 人。空格前为“max”，后接“people”，因此答案应为数字“10”或英文“ten”。虽然提到了通常8人，但题目问的是最大人数，故正确答案是“10 / ten”。
题干询问Level 1课程中学到的基础理论内容之一，与“tides”并列。原文中女士说“You learn about things like the weather... and also the tides”，其中“weather”和“tides”都被列为理论学习内容。因此，“weather”直接对应空格所缺信息，且符合“ONE WORD”的要求。
题目要求填写基本帆船技能中包含的信息类型。原文提到教练会确保学员理解“everything you need to know about safety”，这说明安全知识是课程的重要组成部分。空格前为“basic sailing skills including”，后接“information”，因此填入“safety”最准确，且与上下文逻辑一致。
题干指出俱乐部会员可享某种优惠。原文中女士明确表示“There's a discount for members”，即会员享有折扣。空格前为“GBP200;”，后接“available for club members”，语法和语义均要求填入名词单数形式，“discount”完全匹配，且是唯一提及的会员福利。
题目问费用包含的一项有用物品。原文提到“you also get a really good dictionary explaining all the sailing terminology”，说明费用包含一本术语词典。空格前为“a useful”，需填入单数名词，“dictionary”既符合语境，也与“explaining terminology”形成逻辑呼应。
题干要求填写课程结束时所有参与者获得的东西。原文明确说“on completion of the course you get a certificate”，即完成课程后会获得证书。空格位于“a”之后、“at the end of the course”之前，需填入单数名词，“certificate”完全对应，且是唯一提及的结业凭证。

groups.desc:
Questions 7-10
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

content: Participants must be able to swim.
Bring suitable clothing, a $22153$ and toiletries (e.g. shampoo).
There is a $22154$ at the club.

questions.analysis: 题目要求填写参与者需携带的物品，与“toiletries”并列。男士问“And a towel?”，女士回答“Yes definitely”，确认毛巾是必需品。空格前为“a”，后接“and toiletries”，语法要求单数可数名词，“towel”直接来自对话，且符合“ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER”的格式要求。
题干询问俱乐部内提供食物饮料的场所。原文中女士说“You can get sandwiches, cakes and snacks there”，而前文男士问的是“is there a café at the club?”，说明“there”指代的就是café。因此，“cafe / café”是唯一符合语境的答案，且拼写形式在雅思中两种均可接受。
题目要求填写推荐的在线培训材料。女士建议男士“watch some videos we use for training”，并称它们“available online”。空格前为“Online training”，后无限定词，需填复数名词，“videos”直接对应原文，且是唯一提到的在线准备资源。
题干询问供课程参与者使用的设施。男士问是否有“lockers for people to use”存放贵重物品，女士回答“Yes, there are plenty in the changing rooms”。空格位于句首，需填复数名词作主语，“Lockers”准确反映对话内容，且首字母大写符合表格标题风格。

transcript.en: PART 1
You will hear a man phoning a sailing club to ask about learning to sail.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一名男子致电帆船俱乐部咨询学习帆船的事宜。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 6.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第1至第6题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 1 to 6.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第1至第6题。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Hello, Oyster Bay Sailing Club. How can I help you?
transcript.cn: 女士：你好，牡蛎湾帆船俱乐部，请问有什么可以帮到您？

transcript.en: MAN: Oh hi. I'd like to find out about sailing courses for beginners.
transcript.cn: 男士：你好，我想了解一下初学者的帆船课程。

transcript.en: WOMAN: No problem. Is it for yourself?
transcript.cn: 女士：没问题，是为您自己报名吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Yes. I had a look online but I'm not sure which course would be best.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，我在网上看了一下，但不确定哪个课程最适合我。

transcript.en: WOMAN: OK. Well you might be interested in our Taster Days?
transcript.cn: 女士：好的，您可能对我们的体验日活动感兴趣？

transcript.en: MAN: Possibly.
transcript.cn: 男士：有可能。

transcript.en: WOMAN: So these are for people who've never sailed before – it's basically an introduction to sailing to find out whether you enjoy it and want to carry on with it.
transcript.cn: 女士：这些活动是为从未学过帆船的人设计的——基本上是帆船入门体验，看看您是否喜欢并想继续学习。

transcript.en: MAN: And how much is that?
transcript.cn: 男士：那费用是多少？

transcript.en: WOMAN: It's £120 for the day – but it's reduced to £110 each if there are two of you.
transcript.cn: 女士：一天120英镑，如果两人同来，每人优惠至110英镑。

transcript.en: MAN: No, it would just be me.
transcript.cn: 男士：不，只有我一个人。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Oh that's fine. You'd be in a small group, usually about eight people but no more than ten – and everyone's always very friendly.
transcript.cn: 女士：没关系，您会在一个小组里，通常约八人，最多不超过十人——大家都非常友好。

transcript.en: MAN: Uh huh. And are there any other suitable courses?
transcript.cn: 男士：好的，还有其他合适的课程吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: The other option is the Level 1 course. These are two-day weekend courses and we run those all year round.
transcript.cn: 女士：另一个选择是一级课程，这是两天的周末课程，我们全年都有开设。

transcript.en: MAN: OK. And what do you learn on that course?
transcript.cn: 男士：好的，那个课程学些什么？

transcript.en: WOMAN: This is a mix of theory and practical skills. So you learn about things like the weather, which is obviously really important and also the tides, as well as learning basic sailing skills.
transcript.cn: 女士：这是理论与实践技能的结合，您会学习天气知识——这显然非常重要——以及潮汐知识，还有基本的帆船技能。

transcript.en: WOMAN: You go out into the harbour in special training dinghies for beginners, two people in each dinghy and an instructor. He or she will make sure you understand everything you need to know about safety.
transcript.cn: 女士：您会乘坐专为初学者设计的训练小艇驶入港湾，每艘小艇两名学员，配有一名教练，教练会确保您了解所有必要的安全知识。

transcript.en: MAN: It sounds like hard work!
transcript.cn: 男士：听起来很辛苦！

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes, but you'll have a lot of fun too.
transcript.cn: 女士：是的，不过您也会玩得很开心的。

transcript.en: MAN: And the cost of that one is…?
transcript.cn: 男士：那这个课程的费用是……？

transcript.en: WOMAN: £200. But it's a bit cheaper if you decide to join the club. There's a discount for members.
transcript.cn: 女士：200英镑。但如果您决定加入俱乐部，会便宜一些，会员有折扣。

transcript.en: MAN: Well, I'm not sure about that yet.
transcript.cn: 男士：嗯，这个我还不确定。

transcript.en: WOMAN: You've got plenty of time to decide.
transcript.cn: 女士：您有充足的时间考虑。

transcript.en: MAN: And does the cost include everything?
transcript.cn: 男士：费用包含所有东西吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes, everything's included and you also get a really good dictionary explaining all the sailing terminology. A lot of people struggle with this at first. It's got lots of pictures, so I'm sure you'd find it really helpful.
transcript.cn: 女士：是的，一切都包含在内，您还会获得一本非常好的词典，解释所有帆船术语。很多人起初都觉得这很困难，词典里有很多图片，我相信您会觉得非常有帮助。

transcript.en: WOMAN: And on completion of the course you get a certificate. Then you're ready to move on to the Level 2 course.
transcript.cn: 女士：完成课程后，您会获得一张证书，然后就可以继续学习二级课程了。

transcript.en: MAN: Sounds good.
transcript.cn: 男士：听起来不错。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 7 to 10.
transcript.cn: 在你听剩余对话之前，你有一些时间查看第7至第10题。

transcript.en: Now listen and answer questions 7 to 10.
transcript.cn: 现在请听录音，并回答第7至第10题。

transcript.en: WOMAN: I think that's all the info you need for now. Just a couple of general things. For example, it's really important that you know how to swim.
transcript.cn: 女士：我想这就是您目前需要了解的全部信息了，还有几件一般性的事情，比如，会游泳非常重要。

transcript.en: MAN: Yes, I'm pretty confident in the water.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，我在水里很有把握。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Great. The other thing I should tell you is that we provide wetsuits and life jackets but you need to bring swimming trunks and some old trainers.
transcript.cn: 女士：很好。另外我需要告诉您，我们提供潜水衣和救生衣，但您需要自带泳裤和一双旧运动鞋。

transcript.en: MAN: And a towel?
transcript.cn: 男士：还有毛巾吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes definitely. And you might want to bring your own toiletries, things like shampoo.
transcript.cn: 女士：是的，一定要带。您可能还想带自己的洗漱用品，比如洗发水。

transcript.en: MAN: OK. What about food and drink? Do I need to bring that or is there a café at the club?
transcript.cn: 男士：好的，那食物和饮料呢？我需要自带还是俱乐部有咖啡厅？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes, you can get sandwiches, cakes and snacks there. The food's pretty reasonable.
transcript.cn: 女士：是的，那里可以买到三明治、蛋糕和零食，食物价格相当实惠。

transcript.en: MAN: OK good. Well I think I'm interested in the Level 1 course. But I know absolutely nothing about sailing so is there anything I can do to prepare myself a bit?
transcript.cn: 男士：好的。我想我对一级课程感兴趣。但我对帆船一窍不通，有什么可以提前做些准备的吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: I recommend you watch some videos we use for training. They're available online. I can send you the link. They'll give you an idea of what to expect.
transcript.cn: 女士：我建议您观看一些我们用于培训的视频，可以在网上找到，我可以把链接发给您，视频会让您对课程内容有所了解。

transcript.en: MAN: Perfect, thanks. That would be very helpful. Oh and just one other thing – I'll be cycling to the club and will need somewhere to put valuables. I'm just wondering if there are lockers for people to use?
transcript.cn: 男士：太好了，谢谢，那会很有帮助的。哦，还有一件事——我会骑自行车去俱乐部，需要有地方存放贵重物品，请问有供人使用的储物柜吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes, there are plenty in the changing rooms.
transcript.cn: 女士：是的，更衣室里有很多。

transcript.en: MAN: Great. OK well could you book me onto…
transcript.cn: 男士：太好了，那您能帮我预订……

groups.title: Working as a makeup trainee
groups.desc:
Questions 11-16
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: What should trainees always expect to get when working on low budget short films?
questions.analysis: 题干询问在低预算短片工作中，学徒应始终期待获得什么。原文明确指出“Make sure your transport costs are covered”，即交通费用应被覆盖，这与选项A“travel expenses”形成直接同义替换。同时，原文提到“there's very unlikely to be any catering provided”，排除了C选项“meals”；而“working for free”说明没有工资，排除B选项。因此正确答案是A。

questions.title: According to the speaker, on big budget films trainees may get experience of

options.title: C
options.content: creating a variety of hair styles.

questions.analysis: 题目问在大制作电影中，学徒可能获得哪种经验。原文第11句提到“you're likely to get the opportunity to work with a range of age groups, as well as different ethnicities”，明确支持选项B“working with different ethnicities”。而A选项“makeup for special effects”被原文否定（“don't expect to be offered any practical experience in that”），C选项“hair styles”虽提及发型部门独立，但未说学徒会参与，故排除。

questions.title: The speaker says a problem for makeup artists is

options.title: B
options.content: being shouted at by their supervisor.

options.title: C
options.content: waiting around for hours doing nothing.

questions.analysis: 题干问化妆师面临的问题是什么。原文第12-13句指出工作环境“high-pressure”，并举例“directors who are impatient, or unhappy with the makeup artist's work”，这与选项A“dealing with difficult directors”完全对应。选项C“waiting around doing nothing”与原文“There are very few times when you'll be bored”矛盾；B选项“被主管责骂”未被提及，因此A为正确答案。

questions.title: How did the speaker feel when she met famous actors for the first time?
questions.analysis: 题目问说话人初次见到著名演员时的感受。原文第14句描述“I found it overwhelming and could hardly speak, I was so in awe”，表现出极度紧张和敬畏，对应选项A“very shy”。选项B“proud”和C“disappointed”在原文中均无依据，且后文提到“most actors don't look that special without all the makeup”，也未体现失望情绪，故选A。

questions.title: What advice does the speaker give about makeup kits?

options.title: A
options.content: Always carry a basic kit with you.

options.title: B
options.content: Only buy the best products for a makeup kit.

options.title: C
options.content: Ask other makeup artists to check your kit.

questions.analysis: 题干问关于化妆工具包的建议。原文第16句强调“Every makeup trainee will need a makeup kit... have with them at all times. Just the essentials will do”，说明应随时携带基础工具包，对应选项A“Always carry a basic kit with you”。选项B“只买最好的产品”和C“请他人检查工具包”均未在原文中出现，因此A为正确答案。

questions.title: What advice does the speaker give about creating a portfolio?

options.title: B
options.content: Only include a small selection of photos.

questions.analysis: 题目问关于创建作品集的建议。原文第19句明确指出“You'll need to get approval from the makeup designer”，即使用照片前需获得许可，对应选项C“Get permission to use photos”。选项A“保留纸质和数字照片”部分正确，但原文第20句说明“won't need to get photos printed”，且第25句提到打印硬拷贝仅用于归档，并非作品集本身，故A不准确；B未被提及，因此选C。

groups.desc:
Questions 17-20
What ability is required for each of the following duties?
Write the correct letter, A, B, or C, next to Questions 17-20.

questions.title: Prepping an actor
questions.analysis: 题目问“Prepping an actor”需要哪种能力。原文第23-24句提到有时需“step in and get this done without wasting any time, otherwise the makeup artist will get behind schedule”，强调必须快速完成准备工作，避免延误，这与选项C“working quickly”直接对应，因此选C。

questions.title: Continuity
questions.analysis: 题目问“Continuity”工作所需的能力。原文第25-26句说明学徒需“take photos, log them digitally and print out a hard copy... kept in good order”，以便数月后重拍时能复现妆容，这要求良好的条理性和组织能力，对应选项A“being well-organised”，因此选A。

questions.title: General
questions.analysis: 题目问“General”杂务所需的能力。原文第27句提到“doing anything from getting the teas and coffees to putting on a wash”以及“being willing to do whatever is asked of you”，表明需灵活应对各种临时任务，对应选项B“being flexible”，因此选B。

questions.title: Applying makeup
questions.analysis: 题目问“Applying makeup”（针对群演）所需的能力。原文第28句指出“If there are dozens of extras... you'll need to keep up a swift pace and not spend too long on each person”，强调速度和效率，对应选项C“working quickly”，因此选C。

transcript.en: PART 2
You will hear a woman giving advice on a podcast about how to become a makeup artist in the film industry.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一位女士在播客中介绍如何成为电影行业的化妆师。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 16.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第11至第16题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 16.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第11至第16题。

transcript.en: MAN: Hello and welcome to the film making podcast. In this week's episode, Claire Lemarre talks to us about how to become a makeup artist. Claire's been working as a makeup artist in the film industry for over 20 years and has lots of useful advice about how to get started.
transcript.cn: 男士：你好，欢迎收听电影制作播客。本周节目中，克莱尔·勒马尔将与我们聊聊如何成为一名化妆师。克莱尔在电影行业担任化妆师已有20多年，她有很多关于如何入行的实用建议。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Thanks Ian. Well, before you can become a makeup artist on films you have to spend about 2 years working as a makeup trainee.
transcript.cn: 女士：谢谢你，伊恩。在成为电影化妆师之前，您需要花大约2年时间担任化妆实习生。

transcript.en: A good place to get your first job would be on a low budget short film. Of course, this means that you'll be working for free. But it's often worth it for the experience.
transcript.cn: 获得第一份工作的好地方是低成本短片剧组。当然，这意味着您将无偿工作，但为了积累经验，这通常是值得的。

transcript.en: Make sure your transport costs are covered – and remember, there's very unlikely to be any catering provided, so bring plenty of food.
transcript.cn: 确保交通费用得到报销——记住，几乎不会提供餐饮，所以要多带些食物。

transcript.en: If you're lucky, you might start out on a big budget film where you'll get the most useful experience.
transcript.cn: 如果幸运的话，您可能从大制作电影起步，从中获得最宝贵的经验。

transcript.en: On productions like this, makeup and hair styling are separate departments – so you won't need to bring your curling tongs!
transcript.cn: 在这类制作中，化妆和发型是不同的部门——所以您不需要带卷发棒！

transcript.en: But you're likely to get the opportunity to work with a range of age groups, as well as different ethnicities. Doing makeup for special effects is highly specialised, so don't expect to be offered any practical experience in that.
transcript.cn: 但您很可能有机会与不同年龄层和不同族裔的演员合作。特效化妆是高度专业化的领域，所以不要期望在这方面获得实践机会。

transcript.en: One problem with working in the makeup department is that it's a high-pressure environment. There are very few times when you'll be bored or have nothing to do.
transcript.cn: 在化妆部门工作的一个问题是，那是一个高压环境，您几乎不会有无聊或无事可做的时候。

transcript.en: It can be stressful but you'll see that the top makeup artists are very professional – even when they're having to work with directors who are impatient, or unhappy with the makeup artist's work. Follow your supervisor's lead and try to remain calm at all times.
transcript.cn: 这可能很有压力，但您会发现顶尖化妆师非常专业——即使在面对不耐烦或对化妆工作不满意的导演时也是如此。跟随主管的引导，始终保持冷静。

transcript.en: I've worked with many very famous actors over the years. At first, I found it overwhelming and could hardly speak, I was so in awe. That's preferable, by the way, to becoming too excited and asking for selfies.
transcript.cn: 多年来，我与许多非常著名的演员合作过。起初，我感到不知所措，几乎说不出话来，太敬畏了。顺便说一句，这比过于兴奋、要求自拍要好得多。

transcript.en: Now meeting the talent is just a normal part of the job and to be honest most actors don't look that special without all the makeup!
transcript.cn: 现在与演员见面只是工作的一部分，说实话，大多数演员不化妆时并没有那么特别！

transcript.en: Every makeup trainee will need a makeup kit, which they'll be expected to have with them at all times. Just the essentials will do for the kinds of tasks you'll be given – it won't be anything complicated.
transcript.cn: 每位化妆实习生都需要一个化妆工具包，随时携带。您承担的任务只需要基本工具就够了——不会有什么复杂的东西。

transcript.en: It's worth looking at what the other makeup artists have in their kits – but whatever you do, don't borrow anything without asking first.
transcript.cn: 看看其他化妆师的工具包里有什么是值得的——但无论如何，未经询问不要借用任何东西。

transcript.en: It's very important to build your portfolio. You should take photos of all the work you do and ideally show the different stages of makeup application if you can.
transcript.cn: 建立作品集非常重要。您应该给所有工作拍照，如果可以的话，最好展示化妆的不同阶段。

transcript.en: But remember you'll need to get approval from the makeup designer in charge of the department.
transcript.cn: 但记住，您需要获得负责该部门的化妆设计师的批准。

transcript.en: As you'll be sending your portfolio digitally, you won't need to get photos printed.
transcript.cn: 由于您将以数字方式发送作品集，不需要打印照片。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the podcast, you have some time to look at questions 17 to 20.
transcript.cn: 在你听播客剩余部分之前，你有一些时间查看第17至第20题。

transcript.en: WOMAN: So what does a makeup trainee actually do? You need to think about whether you're the right kind of person to do the job and whether you'd enjoy it.
transcript.cn: 女士：那么化妆实习生实际上做什么呢？您需要考虑自己是否适合这份工作，以及是否会享受它。

transcript.en: You may be asked to help prep an actor ready for makeup. Some actors will arrive having already cleansed and moisturized their skin.
transcript.cn: 您可能会被要求协助演员做好化妆前的准备工作。有些演员到达时已经做好了洁面和保湿。

transcript.en: But sometimes you'll need to step in and get this done without wasting any time, otherwise the makeup artist will get behind schedule.
transcript.cn: 但有时您需要介入并迅速完成这些工作，不能浪费时间，否则化妆师的进度会落后。

transcript.en: Trainees play a useful role in continuity. It will be your responsibility to take photos, log them digitally and print out a hard copy to put in each actor's file.
transcript.cn: 实习生在连续性工作中发挥着重要作用。您的职责是拍照、数字化记录，并打印纸质副本放入每位演员的档案中。

transcript.en: This information needs to be kept in good order as a reshoot can mean replicating makeup months later.
transcript.cn: 这些信息需要妥善保存，因为补拍可能意味着数月后需要复制相同的化妆效果。

transcript.en: General duties mean doing anything from getting the teas and coffees to putting on a wash. Having a positive attitude and being willing to do whatever is asked of you will help you get your next film job.
transcript.cn: 日常职责包括从端茶送咖啡到洗衣服等各种事情。保持积极的态度，愿意做任何被要求的事，将有助于您获得下一份电影工作。

transcript.en: You won't be asked to apply makeup to any of the principal cast, only the extras. If there are dozens of extras involved you'll need to keep up a swift pace and not spend too long on each person. It takes quite a lot of confidence to be able to do this well.
transcript.cn: 您不会被要求给主演化妆，只给临时演员化妆。如果有几十名临时演员，您需要保持快速的节奏，不能在每个人身上花费太长时间。做好这件事需要相当大的自信。

transcript.en: OK now about terms and conditions….
transcript.cn: 好的，现在来谈谈条款和条件……

groups.title: Which TWO features of the lecture on ocean biodiversity had the greatest impact on the students?
groups.desc:
Questions 21 and 22
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: B
list.content: the broad focus of the examples

collect_option.title: Which TWO features of the lecture on ocean biodiversity had the greatest impact on the students?

questions.analysis: 题目要求选出讲座中给学生留下最深印象的两个特点。原文中Phil提到讲座所举的例子是在全球层面（global level），对应选项B“the broad focus of the examples”；Lucy则强调讲座聚焦于长期性问题（long-term issues），对应选项D“the type of issues discussed”。这两个点都被学生明确肯定，因此正确答案是B和D。
本题与第21题共用题干，同样考查讲座中最打动学生的两个方面。听力中Phil指出讲座例子具有全球范围的广度，Lucy则赞赏其对长期议题的关注，分别对应选项B和D。其他选项如A、C、E在对话中未被强调为“印象深刻”的核心特征，因此排除。

groups.title: Which TWO details about the research project particularly impressed the students?
groups.desc:
Questions 23 and 24
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: E
list.content: the large range of specialists involved

collect_option.title: Which TWO details about the research project particularly impressed the students?

questions.analysis: 题目询问研究项目中令学生印象深刻的两个细节。Lucy提到团队由来自不同背景的众多专家组成，对应选项E“the large range of specialists involved”；Phil则称赞讲座中关于追踪海洋生物系统和机器人技术的描述，对应选项C“the use of new technology”。这两点均被明确表达为积极评价，故选C和E。
此题与第23题共享题干，答案逻辑一致。学生对研究项目的赞赏集中在两点：一是多领域专家合作（E），二是新技术如机器人在海洋研究中的应用（C）。原文中“so many experts from such different backgrounds”和“improvements in systems... robots”直接支持这两个选项，其他选项未被提及或强调。

groups.title: Opinions
groups.desc:
Questions 25-30
What is the students' opinion of each of the following resources related to ocean biodiversity?
Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-H, next to Questions 25-30.

list.title: A
list.content: This is aimed at a very specialist audience.

list.title: C
list.content: This was an effective description of a new danger.

list.title: D
list.content: This suggests possible ways to improve the situation.

list.title: E
list.content: This does not give a balanced account.

list.title: F
list.content: This is too predictable to be useful.

list.title: G
list.content: This gives insufficient evidence for its claims.

list.title: H
list.content: This gives a clear explanation of the problems.

questions.title: Article on invasive lionfish
questions.analysis: 关于入侵狮子鱼的文章，Phil指出作者虽强调其环境危害，但“didn’t really provide much information to explain why”，即缺乏充分证据支撑其主张。这与选项G“gives insufficient evidence for its claims”完全吻合，因此选G。

questions.title: Documentary on microplastics
questions.analysis: Phil提到微塑料纪录片“was made ten years ago”，Lucy也认为它“was showing its age”，因为当时人们对微塑料几乎一无所知，而如今已是常识。这表明该片内容已过时，对应选项B“now rather outdated”，故答案为B。

questions.title: Podcast on ocean pollution
questions.analysis: 两人讨论海洋污染播客时，Lucy说“most of it was stating the obvious”，Phil补充“everybody knows that anyway”，说明内容缺乏新意、过于可预测。这与选项F“too predictable to be useful”一致，因此选F。

questions.title: Book on coastal ecosystems
questions.analysis: Lucy起初觉得《沿海生态系统》这本书“hard going”，但最终“ended up with a much better understanding”，Phil也认为它是“well-written summary”且图表很有帮助。这表明该书虽难但解释清晰，对应选项H“gives a clear explanation of the problems”，故答案为H。

questions.title: Article on metal toxicity
questions.analysis: Lucy称金属毒性文章“way above my head”，Phil也表示“couldn’t follow it”，说明内容过于专业艰深，普通读者难以理解。这与选项A“aimed at a very specialist audience”相符，因此选A。

questions.title: Podcast on floating marine cities
questions.analysis: Lucy认为关于海上漂浮城市的播客“presented a rather one-sided picture”，Phil进一步指出它“focused on how this would benefit people and ignored the effects on the environment”，说明内容不全面、缺乏平衡视角。这对应选项E“does not give a balanced account”，故答案为E。

transcript.en: PART 3
You will hear two marine biology students discussing a lecture on marine biodiversity and conservation from a visiting speaker.
transcript.cn: 你将听到两名海洋生物学学生讨论一位客座讲者关于海洋生物多样性与保护的讲座。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 24.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第21至第24题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第24题。

transcript.en: PHIL: That lecture from the visiting speaker yesterday was good, wasn't it?
transcript.cn: 菲尔：昨天客座讲师的讲座很精彩，不是吗？

transcript.en: LUCY: Yeah. I learned a lot from her about ocean biodiversity. I've already done some reading on it, and I did an assignment on some of the problems associated with it last year, but I especially liked the way her lecture focused on more long-term issues.
transcript.cn: 露西：是的，我从她那里学到了很多关于海洋生物多样性的知识。我之前已经读了一些相关资料，去年还做了一篇关于相关问题的作业，但我特别喜欢她的讲座聚焦于更长远问题这一点。

transcript.en: PHIL: Yes, things that aren't currently receiving widespread attention but are likely to be important in the future. That impressed me too. It wasn't exactly a feel-good conclusion because it's hard to see any real solution for a lot of the problems.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：是的，那些目前还没有得到广泛关注但将来可能很重要的事情。这也让我印象深刻。结论并不令人振奋，因为很多问题很难看到真正的解决方案。

transcript.en: LUCY: No, though she did point to where policy changes could be made to protect our marine and coastal environments.
transcript.cn: 露西：确实，不过她确实指出了可以在哪些方面进行政策变革来保护我们的海洋和沿海环境。

transcript.en: PHIL: Mm. But that's just at a national level. The examples she gave were at a more global level, and they really made it clear to me just how wide-ranging the threats to ocean biodiversity are.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：嗯，但那只是在国家层面。她举的例子更具全球性，让我真正清楚地认识到对海洋生物多样性的威胁是多么广泛。

transcript.en: LUCY: Yes, me too.
transcript.cn: 露西：是的，我也是。

transcript.en: PHIL: The research project she described was impressive, wasn't it? I'd have thought it was quite unusual to have so many experts working together.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：她描述的研究项目令人印象深刻，不是吗？我本以为有这么多专家合作是相当罕见的。

transcript.en: LUCY: Yeah, and from such different backgrounds. Must have been a really exciting team to work with. I'd heard of a couple of them before – they were involved in research way back in 2009 warning about the dangers of ocean pollution.
transcript.cn: 露西：是的，而且来自如此不同的背景，与这样的团队合作一定非常令人兴奋。我之前听说过其中几位——他们早在2009年就参与了关于海洋污染危害的研究警告。

transcript.en: PHIL: But now people are much more aware of that, aren't they?
transcript.cn: 菲尔：但现在人们对此更加了解了，不是吗？

transcript.en: LUCY: I suppose so.
transcript.cn: 露西：我想是的。

transcript.en: PHIL: Another thing about the research is that the team members came from all round the world. Though I suppose that's not unusual nowadays, now everyone can work remotely.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：关于这项研究的另一点是，团队成员来自世界各地。不过我想如今这并不罕见，现在大家都可以远程工作了。

transcript.en: LUCY: Right. I liked the way she didn't bombard us with figures – I mean, they were available, but she focused more on the general points they indicated.
transcript.cn: 露西：对。我喜欢她没有用大量数据轰炸我们——我是说，数据是有的，但她更专注于数据所揭示的总体要点。

transcript.en: PHIL: Mm. And the description of improvements in systems used for tracking marine animals and things like robots were really interesting.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：嗯，关于用于追踪海洋动物的系统改进以及机器人等内容的描述真的很有趣。

transcript.en: LUCY: Yes, and her description of how robotics can be used to investigate threats to biodiversity.
transcript.cn: 露西：是的，还有她关于如何利用机器人技术调查生物多样性威胁的描述。

transcript.en: PHIL: Absolutely.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：完全同意。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 25 to 30.
transcript.cn: 在你听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间查看第25至第30题。

transcript.en: Now listen and answer questions 25 to 30.
transcript.cn: 现在请听录音，并回答第25至第30题。

transcript.en: PHIL: While you're here, can we talk about the list of resources we have to evaluate for the seminar tomorrow. I've had a look at them all, but it's been a bit of a rush.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：既然你在，我们能谈谈明天研讨会需要评估的资源清单吗？我都看了，但有点仓促。

transcript.en: LUCY: Yeah. What did you think of that article on invasive lionfish? The one claiming they were expanding their habitat throughout the Mediterranean Sea.
transcript.cn: 露西：是的，你觉得那篇关于入侵狮子鱼的文章怎么样？就是那篇声称它们正在整个地中海扩张栖息地的文章。

transcript.en: PHIL: Well, the writer went on about how dangerous they were in environmental terms, which is probably true, but he didn't really provide much information to explain why.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：嗯，作者大篇幅讲述了它们在环境方面的危害性，这可能是真的，但他并没有提供太多信息来解释原因。

transcript.en: LUCY: I know what you mean.
transcript.cn: 露西：我明白你的意思。

transcript.en: PHIL: I watched the documentary on microplastics, at least I started to, but then I found it was made ten years ago so I gave up.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：我看了关于微塑料的纪录片，至少开始看了，但后来发现是十年前拍的，所以就放弃了。

transcript.en: LUCY: I watched to the end but you're right, it was showing its age. People had hardly heard of microplastics then, whereas now everyone knows about them and how dangerous they are.
transcript.cn: 露西：我看到了最后，但你说得对，它已经过时了。那时人们几乎没听说过微塑料，而现在每个人都知道它们以及它们有多危险。

transcript.en: PHIL: Yeah. Did you listen to the podcast on ocean pollution?
transcript.cn: 菲尔：是的，你听了那个关于海洋污染的播客吗？

transcript.en: LUCY: Mm. I didn't get anything out of it though. Most of it was stating the obvious.
transcript.cn: 露西：嗯，我没从中得到什么收获，大部分内容都是在陈述显而易见的事情。

transcript.en: PHIL: Yes, it mentioned pesticides and plastic and things, and it clearly made the point that they were a bad thing, but everybody knows that anyway.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：是的，它提到了农药和塑料等，并明确指出它们是有害的，但这是人人都知道的事。

transcript.en: PHIL: Did you read that book on coastal ecosystems?
transcript.cn: 菲尔：你读了那本关于沿海生态系统的书吗？

transcript.en: LUCY: The one by John Harper? Yes, I found it hard going at first, it went into a lot of detail about things like the effects of offshore windfarms and fish farms, but actually I ended up with a much better understanding of the issues.
transcript.cn: 露西：约翰·哈珀写的那本？是的，我起初觉得很难读，它详细介绍了海上风电场和养鱼场的影响等内容，但最终我对这些问题有了更深入的理解。

transcript.en: PHIL: Yes, I agree and I thought it was a well-written summary of those. And the diagrams helped a lot too.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：是的，我同意，我觉得这是对这些问题写得很好的总结，图表也帮了很大忙。

transcript.en: LUCY: The article on metal toxicity was way above my head, I didn't know anything about how metals from industrial emissions react in the ocean... and I still don't understand it.
transcript.cn: 露西：关于金属毒性的文章对我来说太难了，我对工业排放中的金属在海洋中如何反应一无所知……现在仍然不理解。

transcript.en: PHIL: I gave up reading after the first chapter – I just couldn't follow it.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：我看完第一章就放弃了——根本看不懂。

transcript.en: LUCY: That podcast on floating marine cities was interesting, though it presented a rather one-sided picture, I thought.
transcript.cn: 露西：那个关于海上浮动城市的播客很有趣，不过我觉得它呈现的画面相当片面。

transcript.en: PHIL: Yes, it focused on how this would benefit people and ignored the effects on the environment.
transcript.cn: 菲尔：是的，它专注于这对人类的好处，忽视了对环境的影响。

transcript.en: LUCY: But anyway, shall we ...
transcript.cn: 露西：不管怎样，我们要不……

groups.title: Sources of rubber
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Sources of rubber Three resources which are essential for industrial civilisation - $22177$ - fossil fuels - rubber Natural rubber This mainly comes from the Para rubber tree, now cultivated in South-East Asia. The supply is limited because - the growth of the tree is $22178$ - production cannot easily be adjusted because of increasing or decreasing $22179$ - the tree only grows near the $22180$ - extracting the latex (rubber) is labour-intensive - it is very difficult to $22181$ rubber after production. New threats include - lack of genetic diversity, leading to danger of disease caused by a $22182$ - a shift to the cultivation of palm oil - extreme $22183$ events. Synthetic rubber - may be used for engine parts and cooking utensils - is less $22184$ than natural rubber - is unsuitable for many purposes e.g. the tyres of aircraft. An alternative source of natural rubber - A wild flower (a type of dandelion) has rubber in its $22185$ - It can be grown in many locations and does not require good $22186$

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写工业文明三大必需自然资源之一，与 fossil fuels 和 rubber 并列。听力原文第6句明确指出“one of these is metal”，直接对应答案。虽然原文使用单数形式，但题目接受 metal 或 metals，因在泛指资源类别时两者皆可。此处无干扰项，定位清晰。
题目询问橡胶树生长的特点，导致供应受限。原文第11句提到“It can take eight to ten years... so cultivating them's a slow process”，其中“slow process”直接说明生长缓慢。空格前为“the growth of the tree is”，需填形容词，故“slow”完全匹配语境和语法要求。
题干指出产量难以调整的原因与“increasing or decreasing”相关。原文第12句提到农民可根据“demand for that crop is rising or falling”灵活调整其他作物，但橡胶树因生长期长无法做到。因此，“demand”是导致生产难以调节的关键因素，且为名词，符合填空要求。
题目问橡胶树只能在何处附近生长。原文第14句明确说明“It can't be grown in the northern or southern parts... only around the equator”。空格前为“near the”，需填地理名词，“equator”（赤道）是唯一符合上下文和科学常识的答案，且为ONE WORD。
题干指出橡胶使用后难以进行某动作，与水和玻璃对比。原文第16句提到“we can recycle them [water and glass], but although this is also possible with rubber, it's very difficult”，明确说明橡胶难以回收。“recycle”为动词原形，符合语法及“ONE WORD ONLY”要求。
题目问因基因多样性低而引发疾病的病原体。原文第18句指出“The most dangerous threat is a fungus, which destroyed large numbers of rubber trees in Brazil”，明确将真菌（fungus）列为最危险的威胁。空格前为“a”，需填可数名词单数，“fungus”完全匹配，且无其他病原体被提及。
题干要求填写近年反复发生的极端事件类型。原文第20句提到“repeatedly hit by extreme types of weather”，虽用“types of weather”，但题目仅需一个词，且“weather”已能准确概括极端天气事件（如台风、洪水等）。其他如“events”或“conditions”未被强调，故“weather”最精准。
题目比较合成橡胶与天然橡胶的性能差异。原文第22句明确说“compared with natural rubber, it's not anything like as strong”，直接指出强度不足。空格前为“less”，需填形容词原级，“strong”符合语法和语义，且后文以飞机轮胎为例佐证其强度要求高。
题目问蒲公英含橡胶的具体部位。原文第24句描述“when it's pulled up and its roots cut open, they're found to contain rubber”，明确指出根部（roots）含有橡胶。空格前为“in its”，需填复数名词，“roots”完全匹配，且植物结构中根是常见储胶部位。
题干指出蒲公英不需要良好的什么即可生长。原文第25句提到“they don't need rich soil”，直接对应“soil”（土壤）。空格前为“good”，需填名词，“soil”符合农业语境，且与橡胶树需特定生长条件形成对比，突出蒲公英的适应性强。

transcript.en: PART 4
You will hear part of a science lecture about different sources of rubber.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一段关于不同橡胶来源的科学讲座。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第31至第40题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第31至第40题。

transcript.en: Much of the world now lives in an industrial civilisation. But this has only become possible because we have the necessary natural resources.
transcript.cn: 世界上大部分地区现在生活在工业文明之中，但这只有在我们拥有必要的自然资源的情况下才成为可能。

transcript.en: There are three types of natural resource without which industry could not exist. One of these is metal – without that we'd have no machines and no transportation.
transcript.cn: 有三种自然资源，没有它们工业就无法存在。其中之一是金属——没有金属，我们就没有机器，也没有交通运输。

transcript.en: Another is fossil fuels, which we need to power those machines.
transcript.cn: 另一种是化石燃料，我们需要用它来驱动那些机器。

transcript.en: But there's a third resource that's essential to connect the different parts of a machine together with belts and pipes and shock absorbers, and that is rubber. It's now used in over 40,000 products, from waterproof footwear to surgical gloves.
transcript.cn: 但还有第三种资源，对于用皮带、管道和减震器连接机器各部件至关重要，那就是橡胶。它现在被用于超过40,000种产品，从防水鞋到外科手套。

transcript.en: At present, we have two types of rubber in common use. One is natural rubber, which nearly all comes from the Pará rubber tree. This was originally native to Brazil, but is now cultivated on plantations in South-East Asia.
transcript.cn: 目前，我们常用的橡胶有两种。一种是天然橡胶，几乎全部来自巴拉橡胶树，这种树原产于巴西，但现在在东南亚的种植园中种植。

transcript.en: Recently, however, concern's been growing that supplies may soon be insufficient for the world's needs. So what exactly is limiting the supply of natural rubber?
transcript.cn: 然而，最近越来越多的人担忧供应可能很快无法满足世界需求。那么到底是什么限制了天然橡胶的供应？

transcript.en: Well, for one thing, rubber trees don't just spring up overnight. It can take eight to ten years for a tree to start producing rubber, so cultivating them's a slow process.
transcript.cn: 首先，橡胶树不会一夜之间就长成。一棵树可能需要八到十年才能开始产胶，所以种植过程很缓慢。

transcript.en: And this leads to another problem. With most crops, farmers don't have to think very far ahead, so they can easily change what crop they produce, or how much of a crop they produce, if they find the demand for that crop is rising or falling.
transcript.cn: 这又引发了另一个问题。对于大多数农作物，农民不需要考虑太远的未来，所以他们可以轻松地改变种植的作物种类或数量，如果他们发现该作物的需求在上升或下降的话。

transcript.en: But if you have to plant eight or ten years ahead, that's much harder. And also the rubber tree's very choosy about where it grows.
transcript.cn: 但如果必须提前八到十年规划种植，那就困难得多了，而且橡胶树对生长环境非常挑剔。

transcript.en: It needs the right temperature, the right amount of rainfall, and the right altitude – not too high and not too low. The result is that it can't be grown in the northern or southern parts of the globe, only around the equator.
transcript.cn: 它需要适宜的温度、适量的降雨和适当的海拔——不能太高也不能太低。结果是它不能在地球的南北部种植，只能在赤道附近种植。

transcript.en: Another problem is that the rubber is basically extracted in the same way as it's been done for hundreds of years, and that's by hand, by making small cuts in the trunk of the tree, and putting a little cup there to catch the latex, as the rubber is called. It's very labour-intensive.
transcript.cn: 另一个问题是，橡胶的提取方式基本上与几百年前相同，即用手工方式，在树干上割小口，放置小杯子收集乳胶（即橡胶汁）。这非常耗费劳动力。

transcript.en: And it's not just the initial production that's limiting supplies. With other resources such as water and glass, when we've finished using them we can recycle them, but although this is also possible with rubber, it's very difficult, so that also reduces the amount we have available.
transcript.cn: 而且限制供应的不仅仅是初始生产。对于水和玻璃等其他资源，我们用完后可以回收，但橡胶虽然也可以回收，却非常困难，这也减少了可用量。

transcript.en: And in the last few years, there have been new threats to the supply of natural rubber. One problem is linked to the fact that nearly all the rubber trees in South-East Asia are descended from just a small number of seeds brought from Brazil in the nineteenth century.
transcript.cn: 在过去几年里，天然橡胶的供应出现了新的威胁。一个问题与以下事实有关：东南亚几乎所有的橡胶树都源自19世纪从巴西带来的少量种子。

transcript.en: This means that there's very little genetic diversity among the trees, which in turn makes them very vulnerable to disease. The most dangerous threat is a fungus, which destroyed large numbers of rubber trees in Brazil, and which could cause devastation to plantations worldwide.
transcript.cn: 这意味着这些树的遗传多样性非常低，这反过来使它们非常容易感染疾病。最危险的威胁是一种真菌，它曾在巴西摧毁了大量橡胶树，可能对全球的种植园造成毁灭性破坏。

transcript.en: Another problem is that farmers in South-East Asia are increasingly turning to the cultivation of palm oil, which is easier and more profitable for them.
transcript.cn: 另一个问题是东南亚的农民越来越多地转向种植棕榈油，这对他们来说更容易且利润更高。

transcript.en: And finally, in recent years South-East Asia, like other parts of the world, has been repeatedly hit by extreme types of weather, and this looks likely to continue in the future.
transcript.cn: 最后，近年来东南亚与世界其他地区一样，反复遭受极端天气的袭击，这种情况未来可能还会持续。

transcript.en: However, as well as using natural rubber, it's also possible to make rubber synthetically. This works very well for some purposes, for example, making engine parts, or silicone pots and pans used for cooking.
transcript.cn: 然而，除了使用天然橡胶，也可以合成制造橡胶。这对某些用途非常有效，例如制造发动机零件或用于烹饪的硅胶锅具。

transcript.en: But compared with natural rubber, it's not anything like as strong, and this means it can't replace natural rubber in other products. For example, while a mixture of natural rubber and synthetic rubber works well in car tyres, only natural rubber can stand up to the extreme speeds of aircraft tyres during take-off and landing.
transcript.cn: 但与天然橡胶相比，合成橡胶的强度差远了，这意味着它无法在其他产品中替代天然橡胶。例如，天然橡胶和合成橡胶的混合物虽然在汽车轮胎中效果很好，但只有天然橡胶才能承受飞机轮胎在起降时的极端速度。

transcript.en: So for some time, scientists have been looking for alternative sources of natural rubber. One that's been known about for some time seems initially to be a rather unlikely source. It's a wild plant with yellow flowers that we normally regard as a weed when we see it in our gardens.
transcript.cn: 因此，科学家们一直在寻找天然橡胶的替代来源。其中一种已被知晓一段时间的来源，起初看起来是一个不太可能的来源——它是一种开黄色花朵的野生植物，我们在花园里看到它时通常将其视为杂草。

transcript.en: But when it's pulled up and its roots cut open, they're found to contain rubber.
transcript.cn: 但当它被拔起并将根部切开时，会发现其中含有橡胶。

transcript.en: Now, compared to the rubber tree, dandelions produce relatively small amounts of rubber, but unlike rubber trees, they're very adaptable. They'll grow in all sorts of places, and they don't need rich soil.
transcript.cn: 与橡胶树相比，蒲公英产生的橡胶量相对较少，但与橡胶树不同，它们适应性很强，可以在各种地方生长，不需要肥沃的土壤。

transcript.en: So at present there are several projects underway investigating the possibility of using dandelions as a source of rubber.
transcript.cn: 因此，目前有几个项目正在调查将蒲公英用作橡胶来源的可能性。

transcript.en: Another possibility is a desert shrub grown in Mexico and Texas…
transcript.cn: 另一种可能性是一种在墨西哥和德克萨斯州生长的沙漠灌木……
